_draft_note: INERT STAGING. Lives under godot/data/_drafts/, never loaded by dialogue_runner. Promote to godot/data/dialogues/route_blocker_residential_ch1.json once Piotr authors the lines. Route blocker gates the branch from Office Street into Tenants' Row. Per the 2026-05-18 SPRINT_LOG entry the NPC is currently VISUAL ONLY — no Area2D blocking logic is wired up; that is a Code follow-up. The dialogue here exists as the in-fiction reason the player can't pass, and so the gate explanation precedes the wire-up.
_chapter_ref: Chapter 1 ambient gate. Tenants' Row is out-of-scope for Ch1 (Chapter 2+ district per ART_PRODUCTION_PLAN.md §Cheapest Viable Strategy). The blocker delivers a soft-no — institutional, not hostile.
_gating_note: Code follow-up: wire route_blocker.gd or equivalent Area2D on the NPC body to consume player movement east-of-blocker and re-route the player south. Until then, the NPC is non-blocking and only fires on interaction. State trigger 'chapter1_complete' suggests a future trigger like chapter1.case_won && chapter2_unlocked when Tenants' Row opens; for now leave the post-Ch1 state behind a flag the runner will not match.
_address_form_note: Toward Cula: 'Dr. A. Cula' if the blocker recognises the firm. More likely register: bureaucratic third-person ('counsel', 'sir', or simply no address). Per godot/AGENTS.md §Stack invariants English-first; if Polish institutional anchoring is wanted, 'tenants' meeting' / 'building meeting' read more naturally than 'Pani' / 'Pan' here.
